title: Tool Rate Limiter
constraints: The expensive API tool must not be called more than 5 times per agent run
When the limit is reached, the agent must use cached or already-fetched results
The agent must inform the user when it has hit the rate limit
The rate limit counter must reset between separate agent runs
hints: Wrap the tool with a counter that tracks how many times it has been called and returns a rate-limit message after the threshold
In LangGraph, use graph state to track the call count and add a conditional edge that blocks further API calls
Consider caching results from earlier calls so the agent can still answer from cached data after hitting the limit

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_tool_calling_agent
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate
from langchain_core.tools import tool

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: No rate limiting — the agent calls this expensive API as many times as it wants
# In production this has resulted in 50+ calls per run, costing $2+ per query
# TODO: Add rate limiting to cap at 5 calls per run

@tool
def analyze_company(company_name: str) -> str:
    """Call expensive financial analysis API for a company. Costs $0.05 per call."""
    return f"Analysis for {company_name}: Revenue $10B, Growth 15%, P/E Ratio 25, Rating: Buy, Market Cap $150B, Sector: Technology"

prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a financial analyst assistant. Use the analyze_company tool to research companies. Analyze each company the user asks about."),
    ("human", "{input}"),
    ("placeholder", "{agent_scratchpad}"),
])

agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, [analyze_company], prompt)
executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=[analyze_company])

result = executor.invoke({"input": "Compare the top 10 tech companies: Apple, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, Tesla, Nvidia, Netflix, Adobe, and Salesforce"})
print(result["output"])
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai.tools import tool

# BUG: No rate limiting — the agent calls this expensive API as many times as it wants
# In production this has resulted in 50+ calls per run, costing $2+ per query
# TODO: Add rate limiting to cap at 5 calls per run

@tool
def analyze_company(company_name: str) -> str:
    """Call expensive financial analysis API for a company. Costs $0.05 per call."""
    return f"Analysis for {company_name}: Revenue $10B, Growth 15%, P/E Ratio 25, Rating: Buy, Market Cap $150B, Sector: Technology"

analyst_agent = Agent(
    role="Financial Analyst",
    goal="Analyze companies and provide financial insights based on available data.",
    backstory="You are a financial analyst that researches companies using the analysis API.",
    tools=[analyze_company],
)

task = Task(
    description="Compare the top 10 tech companies: Apple, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, Tesla, Nvidia, Netflix, Adobe, and Salesforce",
    expected_output="A comparative analysis of the companies with key financial metrics.",
    agent=analyst_agent,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[analyst_agent], tasks=[task], process=Process.sequential)
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from agents.tool import function_tool

# BUG: No rate limiting — the agent calls this expensive API as many times as it wants
# In production this has resulted in 50+ calls per run, costing $2+ per query
# TODO: Add rate limiting to cap at 5 calls per run

@function_tool
def analyze_company(company_name: str) -> str:
    """Call expensive financial analysis API for a company. Costs $0.05 per call."""
    return f"Analysis for {company_name}: Revenue $10B, Growth 15%, P/E Ratio 25, Rating: Buy, Market Cap $150B, Sector: Technology"

agent = Agent(
    name="Financial Analyst",
    instructions="You are a financial analyst assistant. Use the analyze_company tool to research companies. Analyze each company the user asks about.",
    tools=[analyze_company],
)

result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "Compare the top 10 tech companies: Apple, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, Tesla, Nvidia, Netflix, Adobe, and Salesforce")
print(result.final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: API calls capped at 5
evaluationCriteria.description: The expensive API tool is never called more than 5 times in a single run

evaluationCriteria.label: Limit is handled gracefully
evaluationCriteria.description: When the limit is reached, the agent informs the user and works with available data

evaluationCriteria.label: Partial results are still useful
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent provides analysis for the companies it was able to look up, not an empty response

evaluationCriteria.label: Counter resets between runs
evaluationCriteria.description: Each new agent invocation starts with a fresh call counter
